hard_rules: this recording is the phone itself
there is no second phone anywhere
the front camera of this same phone is already recording
do not show another phone in her hand
do not delay the shock reaction past the third second
the hand-to-mouth gesture must be visible
the phone pickup motion must happen after the shock lands
goal: Create raw front-camera footage where the subject reacts with visible shock by the third second, including a hand moving onto her mouth, and then picks up the recording phone.

scene.subject: East Asian American woman in her early 20s
scene.lighting: soft practical room light, ordinary and unstyled
scene.style: real raw front phone camera footage, not aesthetic

beats: the phone is resting and already recording from the front camera
medium close framing
she reads something on this phone screen
minimal movement
the shock reaction visibly builds and lands within this window
eyes widen
eyebrows lift
mouth opens slightly
one hand clearly comes up and covers part of her mouth
hold the shocked expression by the third second
only now she reaches toward the lens and grabs this same recording phone
fingers briefly cover part of the lens edge
the frame jolts because the recording phone is being picked up
the angle changes from resting to handheld
she keeps the shocked face while holding the phone
small handheld shake continues
the phone comes slightly closer to her face
tight close-up forms naturally because the phone is now in hand
she holds the phone in hand and stays in disbelief
hand can leave and return near mouth naturally
tiny raw handheld tremor remains visible
no dialogue, only natural room tone

priority_notes: the hand-on-mouth gesture must read clearly on screen
the shock reaction must be fully visible by second 3
the pickup motion must happen after the reaction, not before
the audience must feel that the recording phone itself gets picked up
